Container.Name: iCloudSync
Container.Overview: Self-hosted service that signs into iCloud, mirrors your Photos library into Postgres, and serves a web UI to browse it and trigger syncs. API, background sync worker, and UI run in one container. Requires a separate PostgreSQL container: set DATABASE_URL to its connection string. Set ICLOUD_ENCRYPTION_SECRET to a long random string (openssl rand -hex 32). Migrations run automatically on first start. Open the WebUI, sign in with your Apple ID, and complete 2FA once.
Container.Description: Self-hosted iCloud Photos backup. Requires an external PostgreSQL container (set DATABASE_URL).

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the web UI and API. Change the left/host value to whatever is free on your server; the WebUI link follows it automatically.

Config.Name: Photos
Config.Description: Where the backed-up original photo files are written. The only persistent app volume — everything else (settings, encrypted session) lives in Postgres.

Config.Name: DATABASE_URL
Config.Description: Postgres connection string for your separate PostgreSQL container. Use the Unraid host LAN IP (or the container name on a shared custom network), NOT localhost.

Config.Name: ICLOUD_ENCRYPTION_SECRET
Config.Description: Secret used to derive the AES key that encrypts the stored iCloud session. Use a long random string (openssl rand -hex 32). Changing it forces a re-login.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID that should own the backed-up photo files. 99 = Unraid 'nobody'.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID that should own the backed-up photo files. 100 = Unraid 'users'.

Config.Name: UMASK
Config.Description: Permission mask for newly written photo files.
